_schema.description: Aggregated settled-markets ledger across all SportsPredict World Cup matches. Each row's source-of-truth is the 'post_match_results.questions' block in that match's own <match>_<date>.json file - this file is a DERIVED rollup for cross-match analysis, regenerate from those files if they change.

fields.q: question number on SportsPredict's leaderboard for that match
fields.question: short question text
fields.category: our internal classification of the methodology used (see category_definitions below)
fields.rbp: Relative Brier Points awarded for this question (positive = beat crowd)

category_definitions.tier2_realdata: No listed market existed; built a real per-match-data model (ESPN hidden API + footystats) with Poisson/Skellam. UPDATED after QAT-SUI (2026-06-13): n=11, avg -2.04 (was 5/5 +7.96 before QAT-SUI). The pre-QAT-SUI wins AND QAT-SUI's one win (Q3, +19.86) were all SIMPLE FIRST-PASS models (focal team's own recent-match Poisson/Skellam, sometimes blended with a market anchor). QAT-SUI's 5 losses ALL involved an extra layer on top of a first-pass estimate: correlated-error false validation (Q1), vague team-morale narrative shading (Q5/Q8), or large late revisions based on a DIFFERENT team's cross-match sample (Q2/Q6). Lesson: trust the first-pass real-data model; treat any further 'creative' layer as a flag, not a feature. See project_sportspredict_edge_analysis.md.
category_definitions.tier1_market: Direct or near-direct mapping from a verified external market (Smarkets/Polymarket/Kalshi), submitted close to that value.
category_definitions.tier1_market_deviated: A verified external market existed but we submitted a value notably different from it (upward deviation in both observed cases). 1/2, +1.66 avg - worse than undeviated tier1_market.
category_definitions.stats_proxy: No direct market; used season/team stat averages or general base rates (not a full per-match Poisson model) as the estimate.
category_definitions.compound_market_proxy: Compound (AND) question where component probabilities were each market-sourced.
category_definitions.compound_independence: Compound (AND) question estimated via naive P(A)*P(B) independence assumption (with or without a small correlation adjustment).
category_definitions.skellam_market_hybrid: Used a Skellam/Poisson-difference model calibrated against a verified market handicap price.
category_definitions.match_winner_internal_model: 'Will Team X win' question, estimate driven primarily by our internal ordered-logit/Elo model.
category_definitions.match_winner_deferred_market: 'Will Team X win' question, estimate driven primarily by deferring to verified external markets over our internal model.
category_definitions.match_winner_gut_override: 'Will Team X win' question where our researched recommendation (tier1_market / RULE1 avg(crowd,market), ~0.58-0.62) was OVERRIDDEN at submission time by personal conviction toward a near-certain value (BRA-MAR Q8: recommended ~0.60, submitted 1.00). n=1, -51.97 - our worst-ever single RBP. The 100% submission guarantees the maximum possible squared error (1.0) if wrong, with no offsetting benefit (the marginal gain over ~0.90 if right is tiny). See project_crowd_bias_model.md RULE 6.
category_definitions.tier1_market_blended: A direct question-specific market doesn't exist, but related team-specific markets (e.g. team cards O/U lines) can be combined/scaled into a market-implied estimate, optionally cross-checked against ESPN history. n=1 (BEL-EGY Q2, +0.75).

_schema.key_finding: BEL-EGY (2026-06-15, +63.72, 9/10 beat crowd, Belgium 1-1 Egypt) is our 2ND-BEST MATCH EVER. Egypt (15.3% underdog) scored early (19') and led at HT; Belgium's equalizer was an EGYPTIAN OWN GOAL (66'). Headline pattern: EGYPT'S DEFENSE SUPPRESSED BELGIUM'S OUTPUT TO EGYPT'S OWN LEVEL across nearly every stat - fouls EXACT TIE 15-15 (Q1, us=0.46 vs crowd=0.59, +15.47 - this is the SECOND time 'near-identical historical averages -> stay near 50%' produced a literal tie, after JPN-NED's 7-7, VALIDATING this as a real pattern not just a hedge), SOT EXACT TIE 3-3 (Q4, Belgium's avg is 8.2 - their output collapsed by >half; our extreme 0.11 still landed correctly since tie=NO either way), Belgium offsides=0 vs avg 1.8 (Q6, the ONLY loss this match, -1.57 - 5th data point for the 'X 2+ offsides' family, all 5 resolved NO, this is the FIRST time the unshaded Poisson exceeded 0.50 (0.54) and the first small loss - watch item, not yet overriding RULE7's +54.57/5 net record). Arguably the MIRROR of RULE10: instead of a blowout favorite's stats staying high while the loser's collapse, here a RESILIENT UNDERDOG caused the FAVORITE's stats to collapse toward the underdog's level. Out-of-sample test of the crowd-prediction regression (crowd_correlation_analysis.md, crowd~=0.514+0.61*(us-0.5)): predicted crowd values had RMSE=5.8pp vs actual crowd (consistent with/better than the in-sample 7.1pp residual std) - the regression holds up. SWE-TUN (2026-06-15, +17.37, 8/10 beat crowd, Sweden 5-1 Tunisia) prior key finding: Q2 ('Sweden more fouls than Tunisia', -33.9, 3RD-WORST-EVER) is RULE10's origin (n=2 with GER-CUR: in blowouts, winning team fouls >= losing team regardless of historical averages). CIV-ECU (+19.86): Q9 (Sangare 1+ SOT) validated RULE9 (positional priors beat crowd, +RBP despite weak grounding). GER-CUR remains our best match ever: +72.47, 9/10, zero gut overrides.
matches_covered: BRA-MAR (2026-06-14, -16.33, 10/10 questions, 7/10 beat crowd - our best beat-crowd RATE; the entire net loss traces to Q8 alone)
GER-CUR (2026-06-14, +72.47, 9/10 beat crowd - our BEST match ever, zero gut overrides)
JPN-NED (2026-06-14, +0.47, 6/10 beat crowd - near-wash, but Q1/Q7 both turned on a single 88th-minute equalizer, see key_finding)
CIV-ECU (2026-06-14, +19.86, Q1-Q9 confirmed/approx, Q10 status unknown - see key_finding for Q9 player-prop validation)
SWE-TUN (2026-06-15, +17.37, 8/10 beat crowd - Q2 (-33.9, 3rd-worst-ever) is the RULE10 'blowout fouls' finding, see key_finding)
BEL-EGY (2026-06-15, +63.72, 9/10 beat crowd - our 2ND-BEST match ever, near-tie fouls validation + offsides watch-item, see key_finding)
SAU-URU (2026-06-15, -13.75, 7/10 beat crowd - Q7 (-35.66, 4th-worst-ever) was RULE14 violation: P(URU more 2H goals)=0.57 > P(URU more 2H SOT)=0.30 is logically inconsistent)
